headlineSummary: Google Gemini drew the clearest positive discussion, while OpenAI, Anthropic, and Microsoft Copilot were in a close tie with mixed or neutral sentiment and limited evidence behind the weaker support for Anthropic and Copilot [E5] [E6] [E3] [E4].

ranking.summary: OpenAI had mixed-to-neutral sentiment in a close tie, with praise for GPT doing better than Claude and for some search links, but also sharp complaints about ChatGPT quality and pricing [E1] [E2] [E3] [E4] [E6].

Anthropic was part of the close tie with mixed sentiment and limited evidence, as Claude was criticized for trying to answer everything itself while also being praised as pretty great in comparison [E3] [E4].
Microsoft Copilot also sat in the close tie with mostly neutral sentiment and limited evidence, getting credit for correct links but also criticism for adding a presumably fake X account [E6].
Google Gemini had the strongest positive tone, with commenters praising its helpful repair guidance and strong search-quality results, including warnings about fake sites [E5] [E6].

annotations.rationale: The title directly names a GPT model and functions as a release announcement without evaluative language.

evidence.excerpt: The single biggest issue for me with ChatGPT right now is how absolutely awful it sounds in every answer. "Why it matters", "the big picture", "it's not jut you", the awful emphasis, the quotations with rhetorical questions, etc.. I don't know if it's intentional so you can easily spot ChatGPT-generated content on the web? The very first GPT-5 version was good but they ruined it immediately afterwards with "making the personality warmer" and making the same mistakes as 4o. I see now that they even ruined Japanese even though it was one of the best languages supported by ChatGPT (under "Limitations" at the end). I don't use it anymore, immensely disappointed.

annotations.rationale: The commenter directly attacks ChatGPT/GPT-5 output quality and says they stopped using it because of disappointment.

evidence.excerpt: This is definitely something I've noticed GPT does much better than Claude in general. Claude preferences trying to answer everything itself without searching.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says GPT does this task much better than Claude.
Claude is criticized for trying to answer everything itself instead of searching.

evidence.excerpt: I unsubbed because ChatGPT was no longer SOTA. They def got cheap. Reminds me of that graph where late customers are abused. OpenAI is already abusing the late customers. Claude is pretty great.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says ChatGPT is no longer state of the art, says OpenAI got cheap, and accuses it of abusing customers.
The commenter explicitly praises Claude as pretty great in contrast to OpenAI.

evidence.excerpt: This time I conversed entirely with Gemini, sending pictures of the cables and of the components and the motherboard. I'll not soon forget when I plugged in a cable incorrectly and sent an image of that cable to Gemini. Gemini said "It is very important that you stop and unplug that cable immediately... Hopefully the power supply's safety precautions kicked in before any permanent damage occurred."

annotations.rationale: The commenter describes Gemini successfully diagnosing a dangerous mistake and giving urgent, useful corrective advice.

evidence.excerpt: ChatGPT, Google AI mode, Gemini, Grok, Perplexity, and Brave Search "Ask" all linked to the official website, and some added the GitHub repo as well. DuckDuckGo Search Assist linked to just the official GitHub. Google AI mode, Gemini and Grok also explicitly warned about the fake websites. Copilot got the official website and GitHub right, but linked to a presumably fake X account as well. Conclusion: Google, Brave and Mojeek win in search. AI is very good and clearly beats search overall. Google AI mode, Gemini and Grok stand out in quality.

annotations.rationale: ChatGPT is reported to have correctly linked the official website, contributing to the positive overall assessment of AI results.
Gemini is said to link correctly, warn about fake sites, and stand out in quality.
Copilot is credited with getting the official website and GitHub right, but also criticized for linking to a presumably fake X account.
